original: Financial regulators in Britain have imposed a rather unusual rule on the bosses of big banks.
translation_literal: 英国金融监管机构已经对大型银行的老板们施加了一条相当不寻常的规则。

chunks.chinese: 一条相当不寻常的规则

chunks.english: on the bosses of big banks

original: Starting next year, any guaranteed bonus of top executives could be delayed 10 years if their banks are under investigation for wrongdoing.
translation_literal: 从明年开始，如果他们所在的银行因不当行为受到调查，高管的任何保证奖金都可能被推迟 10 年发放。

chunks.relation: 说明 could be delayed 的起始时间

chunks.english: any guaranteed bonus of top executives

chunks.relation: 说明 delayed 的时间长度

chunks.english: if their banks are under investigation for wrongdoing
chunks.chinese: 如果他们的银行因不当行为正在接受调查
chunks.relation: 说明 could be delayed 的条件

original: The main purpose of this “clawback” rule is to hold bankers accountable for harmful risk-taking and to restore public trust in financial institutions.
translation_literal: 这条“追回”规则的主要目的，是让银行家为有害的冒险行为负责，并恢复公众对金融机构的信任。

chunks.english: The main purpose of this “clawback” rule
chunks.chinese: 这条“追回”规则的主要目的

chunks.english: to hold bankers accountable for harmful risk-taking and to restore public trust in financial institutions
chunks.chinese: 让银行家为有害的冒险行为负责并恢复公众对金融机构的信任

chunks.english: to hold bankers accountable for harmful risk-taking
chunks.chinese: 让银行家为有害的冒险行为负责
chunks.relation: 表语中的第一个不定式目的

chunks.chinese: 为有害的冒险行为负责
chunks.relation: 补充说明 bankers 的状态

chunks.english: to restore public trust in financial institutions
chunks.chinese: 恢复公众对金融机构的信任
chunks.relation: 表语中的第二个不定式目的

chunks.chinese: 公众对金融机构的信任

sentence_patterns.explanation: 两个不定式并列作表语，分别说明 clawback rule 的两个目的。

original: Yet officials also hope for a much larger benefit: more long-term decision-making, not only by banks but by all corporations, to build a stronger economy for future generations.
translation_literal: 然而，官员们也期待一个大得多的益处：更多长期决策，不仅由银行作出，也由所有公司作出，以为后代建立一个更强大的经济。

chunks.relation: 作 hope for 的宾语

chunks.english: more long-term decision-making, not only by banks but by all corporations
chunks.chinese: 更多长期决策，不仅由银行作出，也由所有公司作出
chunks.relation: 冒号后解释 larger benefit 的具体内容

chunks.english: not only by banks but by all corporations
chunks.chinese: 不仅由银行而且由所有公司
chunks.relation: 说明 decision-making 的执行者范围

chunks.english: to build a stronger economy for future generations
chunks.chinese: 为了为后代建立一个更强大的经济
chunks.relation: 说明 hope for 这一益处的最终目的

sentence_patterns.explanation: 冒号后的名词短语解释前面的 larger benefit。

sentence_patterns.pattern_form: not only by A but by B
sentence_patterns.explanation: not only...but... 展示长期决策应来自银行和所有公司两个范围。

original: “Short-termism” or the desire for quick profits, has worsened in publicly traded companies, says the Bank of England’s top economist, Andrew Haldane.
translation_literal: 英格兰银行首席经济学家安德鲁·霍尔丹说，“短期主义”，或者说对快速利润的渴望，在上市公司中已经恶化。

chunks.english: “Short-termism” or the desire for quick profits
chunks.chinese: “短期主义”，或者说对快速利润的渴望

chunks.english: says the Bank of England’s top economist, Andrew Haldane
chunks.chinese: 英格兰银行首席经济学家安德鲁·霍尔丹说

chunks.relation: 解释 top economist 的姓名

sentence_patterns.explanation: says... 插入在句末，说明前面判断的说话者。
or 后面的名词短语解释 Short-termism 的具体含义。

original: He quotes a giant of classical economics, Alfred Marshall, in describing this financial impatience as acting like “children who pick the plums out of their pudding to eat them at once” rather than putting them aside to be eaten last.
translation_literal: 他在描述这种金融上的急躁时，引用了古典经济学巨匠阿尔弗雷德·马歇尔的话，把它说成像“把李子从布丁里挑出来马上吃掉的孩子们”那样行事，而不是把它们放在一边留到最后吃。

chunks.english: a giant of classical economics, Alfred Marshall
chunks.chinese: 古典经济学巨匠阿尔弗雷德·马歇尔

chunks.relation: 解释 a giant of classical economics

chunks.english: in describing this financial impatience as acting like children rather than putting them aside
chunks.chinese: 在把这种金融急躁描述为像孩子们那样行动而不是把它们放在一边时

chunks.english: as acting like “children who pick the plums out of their pudding to eat them at once”
chunks.chinese: 为像“把李子从布丁里挑出来马上吃掉的孩子们”那样行事
chunks.relation: 补充说明 financial impatience 的表现

chunks.english: who pick the plums out of their pudding to eat them at once
chunks.chinese: 把李子从布丁里挑出来马上吃掉的

chunks.english: rather than putting them aside to be eaten last
chunks.chinese: 而不是把它们放在一边留到最后吃

sentence_patterns.explanation: Alfred Marshall 解释 a giant of classical economics 的具体身份。

sentence_patterns.pattern_name: 分裂结构 / 间隔结构
sentence_patterns.pattern_form: describing A as B rather than C
sentence_patterns.explanation: describing 的宾语和补足语之间较长，且 rather than 引出对照行为。

original: The average time for holding a stock in both the United States and Britain, he notes, has dropped from seven years to seven months in recent decades.
translation_literal: 他指出，在美国和英国持有一只股票的平均时间，在最近几十年里已经从七年下降到七个月。

chunks.english: The average time for holding a stock in both the United States and Britain
chunks.chinese: 在美国和英国持有一只股票的平均时间

chunks.english: in both the United States and Britain
from seven years to seven months
chunks.relation: 说明 dropped 的变化幅度
说明 dropped 的时间范围

sentence_patterns.pattern_form: 主语, he notes, 谓语
sentence_patterns.explanation: he notes 插在主语和谓语之间，阅读时可先跳过。

original: Transient investors, who demand high quarterly profits from companies, can hinder a firm’s efforts to invest in long-term research or to build up customer loyalty.
translation_literal: 短期投资者，那些要求公司提供高季度利润的人，可能会阻碍一家公司投资长期研究或建立客户忠诚度的努力。

chunks.english: who demand high quarterly profits from companies
chunks.chinese: 要求公司提供高季度利润的

chunks.relation: 说明 demand 的对象来源

chunks.english: a firm’s efforts to invest in long-term research or to build up customer loyalty
chunks.chinese: 一家公司投资长期研究或建立客户忠诚度的努力

chunks.relation: 修饰 efforts 的第一项内容

chunks.english: or to build up customer loyalty
chunks.chinese: 或者建立客户忠诚度的
chunks.relation: 修饰 efforts 的第二项内容

sentence_patterns.explanation: 非限制性定语从句插入主语后，补充说明 transient investors 的行为。

sentence_patterns.pattern_form: to invest... or to build up...
sentence_patterns.explanation: 两个不定式并列说明 firm’s efforts 的具体方向。

translation_literal: 这已经被称为“季度资本主义”。

chunks.relation: 补充说明 This 被称作什么

original: In addition, new digital technologies have allowed more rapid trading of equities, quicker use of information, and thus shorter attention spans in financial markets.
translation_literal: 此外，新的数字技术已经使股票交易更快、信息使用更快，并因此使金融市场中的注意力持续时间更短。

chunks.english: and thus shorter attention spans in financial markets
chunks.chinese: 并因此在金融市场中更短的注意力持续时间

chunks.relation: 说明 attention spans 的范围

sentence_patterns.explanation: 三个名词短语并列作 allowed 的结果，第三项由 thus 引出因果推进。

original: “There seems to be a predominance of short-term thinking at the expense of long-term investing,” said Commissioner Daniel Gallagher of the US Securities and Exchange Commission in a speech this week.
translation_literal: 美国证券交易委员会委员丹尼尔·加拉格尔本周在一次演讲中说：“短期思维似乎占据主导，而这是以长期投资为代价的。”

chunks.english: There seems to be a predominance of short-term thinking at the expense of long-term investing
chunks.chinese: 短期思维似乎占据主导，而这是以长期投资为代价的

chunks.relation: there be 结构中的谓语
there be 结构的真实主语

chunks.english: at the expense of long-term investing
said Commissioner Daniel Gallagher of the US Securities and Exchange Commission in a speech this week
chunks.chinese: 美国证券交易委员会委员丹尼尔·加拉格尔本周在一次演讲中说

chunks.english: Commissioner Daniel Gallagher of the US Securities and Exchange Commission
chunks.chinese: 美国证券交易委员会委员丹尼尔·加拉格尔

sentence_patterns.explanation: said... 说明引语来源，阅读时先处理引号内 there be 结构。

original: In the US, the Sarbanes-Oxley Act of 2002 has pushed most public companies to defer performance bonuses for senior executives by about a year, slightly helping reduce “short-termism.”
translation_literal: 在美国，2002 年的《萨班斯-奥克斯利法案》已经推动大多数上市公司把高级管理人员的绩效奖金推迟大约一年发放，从而略微帮助减少“短期主义”。

chunks.relation: 说明 has pushed 的地点范围

chunks.chinese: 2002 年的《萨班斯-奥克斯利法案》

chunks.english: to defer performance bonuses for senior executives by about a year
chunks.chinese: 把高级管理人员的绩效奖金推迟大约一年
chunks.relation: 补充说明 public companies 被推动去做什么

chunks.chinese: 高级管理人员的绩效奖金

chunks.relation: 说明 defer 的时间幅度

chunks.chinese: 从而略微帮助减少“短期主义”
chunks.relation: 说明 has pushed 的结果

original: In its latest survey of CEO pay, The Wall Street Journal finds that “a substantial part” of executive pay is now tied to performance.
translation_literal: 在其最新的 CEO 薪酬调查中，《华尔街日报》发现，高管薪酬的“相当大一部分”现在与绩效挂钩。

chunks.english: In its latest survey of CEO pay
chunks.chinese: 在其最新的 CEO 薪酬调查中
chunks.relation: 说明 finds 的信息来源范围

chunks.english: that “a substantial part” of executive pay is now tied to performance
chunks.chinese: 高管薪酬的“相当大一部分”现在与绩效挂钩
高管薪酬的“相当大一部分”

chunks.relation: 作 tied to 的宾语

original: Much more could be done to encourage “long-termism,” such as changes in the tax code and quicker disclosure of stock acquisitions.
translation_literal: 可以做更多事情来鼓励“长期主义”，例如税法的变化和更快披露股票收购。

chunks.relation: 说明 could be done 的目的

chunks.english: such as changes in the tax code and quicker disclosure of stock acquisitions
chunks.chinese: 例如税法的变化和更快披露股票收购
chunks.relation: 举例说明 Much more 的具体措施

sentence_patterns.explanation: such as 后列举鼓励长期主义的具体措施。

original: In France, shareholders who hold onto a company investment for at least two years can sometimes earn more voting rights in a company.
translation_literal: 在法国，持有一家公司投资至少两年的股东，有时可以在公司中获得更多投票权。

chunks.relation: 说明 can earn 的地点范围

chunks.english: shareholders who hold onto a company investment for at least two years
chunks.chinese: 持有一家公司投资至少两年的股东

chunks.english: who hold onto a company investment for at least two years
chunks.chinese: 持有一家公司投资至少两年的

chunks.relation: 作 hold onto 的宾语
说明 earn voting rights 的范围

original: Within companies, the right compensation design can provide incentives for executives to think beyond their own time at the company and on behalf of all stakeholders.
translation_literal: 在公司内部，正确的薪酬设计可以提供激励，使高管超越自己在公司的任期并代表所有利益相关者来思考。

chunks.english: incentives for executives to think beyond their own time at the company and on behalf of all stakeholders
chunks.chinese: 激励，使高管超越自己在公司的任期并代表所有利益相关者来思考

chunks.english: for executives to think beyond their own time at the company and on behalf of all stakeholders
chunks.chinese: 使高管超越自己的任期并代表所有利益相关者来思考的

chunks.english: beyond their own time at the company
chunks.chinese: 超越他们自己在公司的时间
chunks.relation: 说明 think 的第一方向

chunks.english: and on behalf of all stakeholders
chunks.chinese: 并代表所有利益相关者
chunks.relation: 说明 think 的第二方向

sentence_patterns.explanation: 两个介词短语并列，说明 executives 思考时需要超越自身任期并考虑利益相关者。

original: Britain’s new rule is a reminder to bankers that society has an interest in their performance, not just for the short term but for the long term.
translation_literal: 英国的新规则是对银行家的一个提醒：社会对他们的表现有利害关系，不只是为了短期，而且是为了长期。

chunks.english: that society has an interest in their performance, not just for the short term but for the long term
chunks.chinese: 社会对他们的表现有利害关系，不只是为了短期，而且是为了长期
chunks.relation: 解释 reminder 的具体内容

chunks.chinese: 对他们表现的利害关系

chunks.english: not just for the short term but for the long term
chunks.chinese: 不只是为了短期，而是为了长期
chunks.relation: 说明 society has an interest 的时间维度

sentence_patterns.explanation: that 从句解释 reminder 提醒银行家的具体内容。
not just...but... 对比短期和长期两个时间维度。
